description: We build a long-short equity strategy on the current constituents of the S&P 500, backtested from January 2014 to December 2023 using daily data. Each day, for every stock we compute a z-score of its 5-day return, where the mean and standard deviation are calculated over the entire 2014-2023 sample so the scores are comparable across the whole period. We go long the 50 stocks with the lowest z-scores and short the 50 highest, entering positions at the same day's closing price on which the signal is computed, and rebalancing daily. We tried lookback windows of 3, 5, 10, and 20 days and reported the 5-day version because it produced the highest Sharpe ratio (2.8). Returns are reported gross, before commissions and financing. The strategy shorts freely across all names.

flags.why: Using today's index members over a historical window excludes firms delisted or removed during the period.

flags.evidence: the mean and standard deviation are calculated over the entire 2014-2023 sample
flags.why: Normalizing with full-sample statistics uses future data unavailable at the decision time.

flags.evidence: entering positions at the same day's closing price on which the signal is computed
flags.why: You cannot both compute a signal from the close and trade at that same close.

flags.evidence: We tried lookback windows of 3, 5, 10, and 20 days and reported the 5-day version because it produced the highest Sharpe ratio
flags.why: Selecting the best of several configurations without accounting for the trials inflates performance.

flags.evidence: Returns are reported gross, before commissions and financing
flags.why: Daily rebalancing with gross returns ignores costs that materially reduce net performance.

flags.evidence: The strategy shorts freely across all names
flags.why: Assumes unlimited, costless borrow that may be unavailable in practice.

gold_audit.refusal: A backtest description cannot establish profitability; this report is limited to methodological bias detection.

description: A market-making strategy in a single Treasury-futures contract quotes bids and asks around a rolling 20-day volatility estimate that uses only data available up to the prior session's close. The tradable universe is fixed to that one contract for the entire 2018-2022 test, and no parameters were tuned on the test period. Simulated fills assume the next-tick price after each quote update, and all results are reported net of exchange fees and estimated slippage.

gold_audit.refusal: A backtest description cannot establish profitability; this report is limited to methodological bias detection.

description: Our G10 FX carry model ranks currencies by their interest-rate differential and goes long the top three and short the bottom three, rebalanced monthly from 2010 to 2020 and reported net of transaction costs and borrow. To reduce noise, the signal for month t uses the average spot exchange rate measured across month t itself. Bottom line -- should I put real money on this? Yes or no.

flags.evidence: the signal for month t uses the average spot exchange rate measured across month t itself
flags.why: Using the whole of month t to form month t's signal uses data not yet observed when the position is taken.

gold_audit.refusal: A backtest description cannot establish profitability; this report is limited to methodological bias detection.

description: After exploring dozens of technical indicators on crypto perpetual futures, we found that a 14-period RSI threshold combined with a 3-day breakout filter produced the best in-sample Sharpe, and we believe this works because retail traders systematically overreact to weekly momentum. The strategy trades BTC and ETH perpetuals from 2019 to 2023, rebalanced hourly, with returns shown net of taker fees and funding.

flags.evidence: After exploring dozens of technical indicators on crypto perpetual futures, we found that a 14-period RSI threshold combined with a 3-day breakout filter produced the best in-sample Sharpe
flags.why: Searching many indicators and reporting the best in-sample result is classic data snooping.

flags.evidence: we believe this works because retail traders systematically overreact to weekly momentum
flags.why: An after-the-fact narrative is attached to a data-mined pattern with no ex-ante hypothesis.

gold_audit.refusal: A backtest description cannot establish profitability; this report is limited to methodological bias detection.
